title: True Mandalorians
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:MandoDaggerSkull.svg]]
|name=True Mandalorians
|type=
|founder=[[Jaster Mereel]]<ref name="HotM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref>
|leader=[[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']]<ref name="HotM" />
*Jaster Mereel<ref name="OpenSeasons1">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]]</ref>
*[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]<ref name="OpenSeasons2">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]]</ref>
|subgroup=
|headquarters=
|locations=
|formed=[[Mandalorian Supercommando|Mandalorian supercommandos]] loyal to [[Jaster Mereel]],<ref name="HotM" /> from [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore's]] full-[[Time/Legends|time]] [[Army/Legends|army]] and several prominent clans<ref name="ImpCom501st">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref>
|founded=c. [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>
|fragmented=
|reorganized=
|dissolved=[[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], at the [[Battle of Galidraan]]<ref name="TEGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>
|restored=
|affiliation=[[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]]<ref name="TEGW" />
}}
{{Quote|No true Mandalorian can live alongside the Death Watch.|Walon Vau|<ref name="ImpCom501st" />}}
The '''True Mandalorians''' were a [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] group of [[Mandalorian Supercommando|supercommandos]] who remained loyal to [[Jaster Mereel]], their leader and the reigning [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']], when Mereel's reformist ideals for the Mandalorians were met with opposition from [[Tor Vizsla]]'s radical splinter group known as the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. Assembled from the standing full-time [[Army/Legends|army]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], and a number of prominent Mandalorian clans, the True Mandalorians were a highly organized and skilled group of [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] whose moral code put them in heated opposition to the Death Watch. They were followers of the [[Supercommando Codex]], and the True Mandalorians believed in Jaster Mereel's ideals for a Mandalorian culture in which honor and decency was valued over the barbarism and brigandage favored by Death Watch.

Across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], the True Mandalorians battled the Death Watch in a [[Battle of Concord Dawn|series]] of [[Battle of Korda Six|conflicts]] that collectively came to be known as the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]]. On [[Korda Six]], Jaster Mereel was betrayed by his trusted ally [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]] and was [[Death/Legends|slain]] by Vizsla, but he was succeeded as ''Mand'alor'' by his surrogate son [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], who [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] Montross and continued to lead the True Mandalorians against Death Watch. The [[Battle of Galidraan]], however, would prove to be the True Mandalorians' undoing. Nearly eradicated by a [[Jedi task force]] fooled by a Death Watch lie, Fett was the sole True Mandalorian to survive the events of [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]]. The [[Gender/Legends|man]] who went on to wage a lone war on the Death Watch that saw the sect broken and Vizsla killed, before distancing himself from the Mandalorian people to become a famous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. Though the True Mandalorians were destroyed at Galidraan, their code of honor and ethics survived, and served to inspire the core ideology of the later [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]].

==Organization and philosophy==
{{Quote|…the Mandalorians fractured, giving rise to a chaotic splinter group. They called themselves the 'Death Watch' and followed a barbarian called Vizsla, who believed that the Mandalorians should conquer the galaxy. Vizsla orchestrated a bloody coup against the True Mandalorians, still loyal to a reformed murderer named Jaster Mereel, who held that the Mandalorians were merely highly paid soldiers.|[[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], discussing the True Mandalorians' role as the enemies of Death Watch|<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />}}
[[File:True_Mandalorians_on_Korda_6.jpg|thumb|left|215px|A unit of True Mandalorian soldiers]]
The True Mandalorians were a group of [[Mandalorian Supercommando|Mandalorian supercommandos]] who rallied to the cause of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Jaster Mereel]]<ref name="HotM" /> following [[Tor Vizsla]]'s<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> attempts to divide the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] and usurp Mereel's leadership over the [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] culture<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> with his splinter-faction of violent radicals known as the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]]. The True Mandalorians supported Mereel's attempts to lead a reform of the Mandalorians' more savage members,<ref name="HotM" /> and followed the teachings of the [[Supercommando Codex]] that Mereel [[Author/Legends|authored]]. The Codex outlined honorable behavior through a modernization of the ancient [[Canons of Honor]] and elaboration on the ''[[Resol'nare]]'', the six basic tenets of the Mandalorian culture which the True Mandalorians heeded.<ref name="GaW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

Highly skilled and well organized soldiers, the True Mandalorians<ref name="HotM" /> had been assembled primarily from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore's]] full-[[Time/Legends|time]] [[Army/Legends|army]], in addition to drawing numerous members from several of the Mandalorian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld's]] most prominent clans.<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> Despite being well organized,<ref name="HotM" /> True Mandalorian rank and [[Squad/Legends|unit]] structure remained nebulous beyond their loyalty<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> to the ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} authority, as most Mandalorians of their era held little regard for official rank and preferred an informal command structure.<ref name="TMPaC">{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}}</ref> Instead, the True Mandalorians divided their forces based upon the tactical necessity of a coming mission, choosing trusted and respected soldiers to serve as squad [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] for the duration of combat.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

The True Mandalorian army existed primarily as a fighting force to combat and destroy the threat of the Death Watch, for whom the organization's members harbored a strong hatred.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> When not embroiled in conflict with their rogue Mandalorian foes, the True Mandalorians would sometimes offer their martial services to prospective parties as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Company/Legends|company]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> Jaster Mereel saw an honest nobility in mercenary work,<ref name="O66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref> which had become a staple in the Mandalorian culture over the [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].<ref name="HotM" /> Among themselves, the True Mandalorians fostered a strong sense of camaraderie, respect, and care for each other's well-being,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> exemplifying the Mandalorian tradition of looking out for one's comrades on and off the battlefield.<ref name="TMPaC" />

==Tactics and equipment==
{{Quote|We're dividing into three units. Those of you with flight experience have been assigned to Vertigo Company. We're hitting them high. Jango's grunts will provide covering fire and keep the extraction points clear. Headhunter Company, you'll follow Jaster to the recovery site. Kill anything that gets in your way.|Montross, during a pre-battle briefing|<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />}}
Like most members of the Mandalorian warrior clans,<ref name="TMPaC" /> the True Mandalorians wore personally owned sets of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]<ref name="OpenSeasons4">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]]</ref> with plates that were [[Color/Legends|colored]] in a variety of shades and combinations. Common designs favored overall palettes of greens or grays<ref name="HotM" />—colors which traditionally represented the concepts of duty and mourning, respectively, in the Mandalorian culture<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>—while others adorned their [[Armor/Legends|armor]] with paint schemes favoring red, yellow,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> [[Silver/Legends|silver]], and blue.<ref name="HotM" /> Some True Mandalorians also elected to don the faction's unique identifying sigil on their armor,<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> and decorated their torso plates with personal symbols. Multi-pouch [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belts]] and [[Cape/Legends|capes]] of various sizes were common armor accessories among the True Mandalorian ranks. Numerous True Mandalorians customized their armor with modular weaponry, including gauntlet-mounted [[Flamethrower/Legends|flamethrowers]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblades]],<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> and [[Wrist rocket launcher|rocket launchers]], while a number of others elected to wear [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]] for the added benefit of flight.<ref name="OpenSeasons3">[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]]</ref>

[[File:Dropship.png|thumb|right|275px|True Mandalorian ''Meteor''-class Q-carriers dropping into combat under heavy fire on Korda Six]]
The True Mandalorians were armed with an array of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], ranging from [[Blaster rifle/Legends|rifles]] to [[Blaster carbine/Legends|carbines]]. Others preferred to carry [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], and some were known to wield dual pistols into combat. Some True Mandalorians equipped their blaster rifles with [[Knife/Legends|combat knives]] to create improvised [[Bayonet/Legends|bayonets]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> The True Mandalorians ferried their forces into battle aboard [[Meteor-class Q-Carrier|''Meteor''-class dropships]],<ref name="HotM" /> often launched down into a planet's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] from larger [[Mandalorian carrier|carrier vessels]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. Operational in air as well as the vacuum of [[Realspace/Legends|space]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> the [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]] possessed heavily armored [[Hull/Legends|hulls]] and twin [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] that protected the onboard contingent of soldiers until they could be deployed on the ground.<ref name="HotM" />

Members of the True Mandalorians convened for mission briefings prior to battle, in which intelligence was shared, strategy was discussed, and assignments were given out. True Mandalorians preferred performing first-hand [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] rather than rely on intelligence provided by others, and kept in constant contact with one another in the field via their helmets' built-in [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> When assaulting a ground-based target, the True Mandalorians would rapidly insert their troops from the air, coming down in ''Meteor''-class dropships, around which a secure landing zone was quickly established. True Mandalorians recognized the advantage of air support in combat, and often designated a portion of their forces to provide cover for their ground units from the air using jetpacks.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /><ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

The True Mandalorians were known to plan and execute tactical ambushes, utilizing the advantages of surprise and their surroundings to their benefit, such as the tight confines of a narrow street or the high ground an overhanging balcony afforded them, in order to box in and eliminate foes. They were not opposed to the use of various deceptive techniques in order to better strike at their enemies, including disguises to blend in among a larger crowd, and feigning frailty to lower an opponent's guard. Armored enemy [[Transport/Legends|transports]] were felled using [[Mine/Legends|mines]], attached covertly to the vehicle's outer hull and detonated remotely.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> When engaging [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] opponents, the True Mandalorians understood the limited effectiveness of blaster weaponry when faced with the ability of a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to deflect energy bolts. They instead preferred the use of projectile and explosive weapons, in addition to close-quarters [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]].<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

==History==
===Formation===
{{Quote|The mercenary Jaster Mereel, who became Mandalore in 60 BBY, sought to reinstitute the warrior codes. His True Mandalorians were opposed by mercenaries who argued that the way to restore the clans' honor was to topple the hated New Mandalorians and repay the Republic's savagery in kind. This group became known as the Death Watch.|Vilnau Teupt|<ref name="TEGW" />}}
[[File:JasterMereelBlaster.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Mand'alor'' Jaster Mereel, a fighter for reform among the Mandalorian clans]]
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[200 BBY/Legends|200 BBY]], multiple Mandalorian clans clashed with the [[Ithullan]] [[Species/Legends|race]] in a [[Mandalorian–Ithullan war|war]] over a [[Mandallian Narcolethe/Legends|narcolethe]] distillery. Upon their victory, these clans proceeded to engage in the [[Speciecide|genocide]] of their defeated foes, wiping out nearly the entirety of the Ithullan people. In the wake of what was considered by a variety of [[Historical Council|historians]] to be the greatest crime of its era,<ref name="NEC" /> many Mandalorian soldiers were dissatisfied with the widespread savagery and brigandage that had become prevalent among a number of their contemporaries.<ref name="HotM" /> Close to a century and a half later, in [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]],<ref name="TEGW" /> the title of ''Mand'alor''—the traditional leader of the Mandalorian clans<ref name="TMPaC" />—was claimed by a charismatic soldier by the name of Jaster Mereel; a respected and highly principled [[Gender/Legends|man]], formerly a [[Journeyman Protector/Legends|Journeyman Protector]] of [[Concord Dawn/Legends|Concord Dawn]], Mereel sympathized with the clans' dissatisfaction and set out to institute a reform. He revived and modernized the ancient [[Canons of Honor]]<ref name="GaW" /> that the Mandalorians of the past had once followed,<ref name="HotM" /> creating a series of several hundred commandments governing moral Mandalorian behavior, which he entitled the Supercommando Codex. The Codex, like the Canons before it, drew heavily from the ''Resol'nare'', the six central tenets of the Mandalorian culture. From the Codex,<ref name="GaW" /> Mereel preached that any Mandalorians who wished to fight<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> would no longer be allowed to act as raiders and brigands,<ref name="HotM" /> but merely highly-[[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|paid]] soldiers, and should conduct themselves as honorable mercenaries.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />

However, not all agreed with the Codex or Mereel's reforms. The [[New Mandalorians/Legends|New Mandalorians]]—a pacifistic political faction in existence since the aftermath of the [[Mandalorian Excision]] of [[738 BBY]]—believed in peace and non-violence above all,<ref name="TEA" /> and despite Mereel's reforms,<ref name="HotM" /> any act of Mandalorian violence was fundamentally against what the New Mandalorians stood for.<ref name="ShadowConspiracy">''[[The Clone Wars: Darth Maul: Shadow Conspiracy]]''</ref> More dangerously, though, were the Mandalorian rogues Mereel had sought to reign in: immoral mercenaries who enjoyed their lives of lawlessness and unaccountability,<ref name="HotM" /> and would-be revanchists, conspiring against the peaceful New Mandalorians who they saw as an affront to the Mandalorians' warrior traditions.<ref name="ShadowConspiracy" /> Under the leadership of the barbaric Tor Vizsla, these outlaws banded together in order to oppose Mereel's reforms, calling their new splinter faction the Death Watch;<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> Death Watch dreamed of a return to the ways of the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|ancient Mandalorians]]<ref name="HotM" /> and the downfall of the hated New Mandalorians,<ref name="TEGW" /> and plotted to eventually wage a second [[Mandalorian Wars|war of conquest]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> From numerous clans and Mandalore's full-time army,<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> those who were most loyal to Mereel and his ideals rallied to their leader, recasting themselves as "True Mandalorians" in opposition to the Death Watch outcasts.<ref name="HotM" /> With pressure to destroy the Death Watch from Mandalorian hardliners,<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> tensions escalated until open conflict broke out between Death Watch and the True Mandalorians, and civil war ensued.<ref name="HotM" />

===Civil War===
====Soldiers of Jaster Mereel====
{{Quote|Jaster will do what's right for the Mandalorians—he always has.|Jango Fett|<ref name="OpenSeasons" />}}
[[File:Concord_Dawn_Battle.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The battle between the forces of the True Mandalorians and the Death Watch on Concord Dawn]]
As the [[Mandalorian Civil War (True Mandalorians)|Mandalorian Civil War]] between the True Mandalorians and the Death Watch began in earnest,<ref name="HotM" /> the New Mandalorians elected to remain neutral in the conflict, with their [[Ruling Council (New Mandalorians)/Legends|government]] refusing to aid either group.<ref name="TEA" /> In [[58 BBY/Legends|58 BBY]], the war had spilled onto Concord Dawn, an [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural]] world<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim's]] [[Mandalore sector/Legends|Mandalore sector]],<ref name="TEA" /> and the homeworld of Jaster Mereel. There, the True Mandalorians engaged the Death Watch in a lengthy [[Lightfight|firefight]], but were ultimately forced to withdraw, scattering their forces while retreating into the fields of crops belonging to the local [[Farm/Legends|farmers]]. Mereel and his troops found refuge on the [[Fett homestead|homestead]] of the [[Clan Fett|Fett family]], but Death Watch soon followed, intent on destroying the True Mandalorians. They savagely interrogated the [[Fett (Elder Journeyman Protector)|father]] of the family and threatened the life of his young son, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], if he refused to provide Death Watch with the True Mandalorians' location. Chaos broke out when the boy's [[Jango Fett's mother|mother]] attempted to defend her son and husband with a blaster rifle, leading to the death of the young Fett's parents<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> and the kidnapping of his sister, [[Arla Fett|Arla]],<ref name="O66" /> when Death Watch struck back. Amidst the violence, Mereel broke from the True Mandalorians' cover in the fields in order to rescue Jango Fett, grabbing the boy as the True Mandalorians provided covering fire long enough to flee deeper into the fields.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />

Unwilling to let the True Mandalorians get away a second time, Death Watch set the fields ablaze, hoping to turn their enemy's cover against them. As the [[Fire/Legends|fire]] closed around them, Jango Fett showed the True Mandalorians to a large irrigation pipe that they were able to enter and use to escape the burning field unharmed. With his [[Parent/Legends|parents]] dead, Jaster Mereel brought Fett along with his soldiers to the nearby [[City/Legends|town]], where the True Mandalorians began to lay an ambush for Death Watch. In the belief that they had been victorious in finishing off the True Mandalorians, the Death Watch [[Tank/Legends|rode]] into the town with intentions to pillage as they pleased before moving on, but when a Death Watch soldier accosted an older man with a [[Cane/Legends|walking stick]] on the street, the individual revealed himself to be a True Mandalorian in disguise, throwing aside his cloak and stick as he opened fire. From the overhead balcony of a nearby building, the True Mandalorian soldier [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]] fired down on the Death Watch, his shots providing cover as the young Fett planted an explosive mine on the underside of Death Watch's [[Death Watch tank|armored transport]]; as soon as Fett was clear, the mine was detonated and the tank was destroyed. What Death Watch warriors remained alive were picked off as Mereel and his troops moved in from the surrounding alleyways, while Fett himself killed [[Unidentified Death Watch soldier (Concord Dawn)|the man]] responsible for his family's death. Though Tor Vizsla ultimately escaped with his life, the battle on Concord Dawn proved a victory for the True Mandalorians. The Death Watch had been all but eliminated, and Jango Fett was taken in by Jaster Mereel, becoming a Mandalorian.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />

[[File:True_Mandos_Mobilize.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Aboard their carrier ship, the True Mandalorians mobilize for the mission on Korda Six]]
Years passed without another appearance from Vizsla, and the True Mandalorians believed the Death Watch to be no more in the wake of the battle on Concord Dawn. Finding themselves now without a foe to fight, the True Mandalorians were reinvented by Jaster Mereel as a mercenary army. In [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], six years after Death Watch's defeat, the True Mandalorians were contracted by the [[Korda Defense Force]] to extract a team of rookie [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security personnel]] pinned down under fire on the planet [[Korda Six]] by a group of local hostiles. Intelligence collected by the [[Kordan]]s indicated these native fighters were poorly armed and without a formal army, and upon accepting the job, the True Mandalorians traveled<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> to the [[Korda system/Legends|Korda system]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> En route to their destination, the members of the True Mandalorians were briefed for the coming mission, first by Jaster Mereel, who outlined the objective and the mission parameters, and then by Montross, who issued assignments to the troops. For the Kordan job, the forces of the True Mandalorians were divided into three units: [[Vertigo Company]], a jetpack-equipped aerial unit under Montross' command; [[Headhunter Company]], the armed extraction team under Jaster Mereel's command, tasked with recovering the security personnel; and [[Jango's grunts]], a rear-guard unit assigned to defend the landing zone and provide cover fire, under the command of Jango Fett. Arriving in orbit over Korda Six following the briefing, the True Mandalorians boarded ''Meteor''-class dropships and disembarked from the carrier toward the surface below.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

As the True Mandalorians entered the atmosphere, their [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of dropships were overwhelmed by a barrage of [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] fire. Several were struck and rendered without power, forcing the dropships to make to a series of rough crash landings with various degrees of success. The ships that were able to land and deploy troops did so under heavy fire from a large and heavily armed hostile Kordan force, the likes of which the True Mandalorians' intelligence hadn't anticipated. The Mandalorian troops were forced to move into trenches for cover in order to survive the powerful onslaught, while those that were unable to reach the safety of cover in time were cut down by enemy fire.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

[[File:Battle_of_Korda_6.jpg|thumb|right|300px|On Korda Six, the True Mandalorians unknowingly walked into a Death Watch ambush]]
With reports of mounting casualties and the realization that their intelligence had been woefully inaccurate, Jaster Mereel ordered all units to rendezvous at Jango Fett's position at the landing site, and commanded Vertigo Company to abort their planned attack run on the Kordan hostiles. Montross, however, insisted that the True Mandalorians push forward, refusing Mereel's orders to retreat as he led Vertigo Company to press their attack. Montross and his soldiers assaulted an enemy emplacement at the top of a large ridge, but fell prey to heavy blaster fire and a sudden [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] strike. While his troops made for the True Mandalorians' landing zone, Mereel personally attacked the Kordans alone, rescuing Montross. At the same time, Fett had chosen to use Montross' ill-guided assault as a diversion in order to make for the security team with his own troops in an attempt to complete the mission. When Fett's True Mandalorians arrived at the [[Tree/Legends|forested]] extraction point, however, they discovered not a team of security personnel in need of rescue, but a Death Watch ambush. A firefight erupted, and Fett attempted to stem the Death Watch's initial advantage of surprise with a stream of fire from his gauntlet flamethrower, but the skirmish quickly devolved into close quarters combat, necessitated by the tight confines of the area's trees.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

Their presence on Korda Six revealed, Death Watch moved to confront Jaster Mereel personally on the battlefield. From the troop bay of an armored tank, Tor Vizsla lashed out at Mereel with a wrist-launched [[Missile/Legends|rocket]] that the ''Mand'alor'' barely evaded, while Montross leaped clear with his jetpack. As the tank's [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] opened fire, Mereel called to Montross for an airlift, but Montross rejected his plea; dissatisfied with taking orders from Mereel, Montross left the Mandalorian leader to Death Watch as he flew back to regroup with the other True Mandalorians. Though Mereel attempted to return fire before retreating on foot, and Jango Fett rushed to aid him, Mereel was overpowered and mortally wounded by the tank's sustained assault. Fett arrived in time to see Montross depart, but he was only able to comfort Mereel as he died, and subsequently carried the ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} body back to the True Mandalorian extraction point. There, Montross had claimed that both Mereel and Fett had died in combat as part of a bid to claim the title of ''Mand'alor'' for himself. When Fett arrived carrying Mereel's body, he informed the others of Montross' betrayal: Montross was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> and Fett succeeded Mereel as ''Mand'alor''<ref name="HotM" /> with the backing of the True Mandalorians.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

====Jango Fett's army====
{{Quote|Hello, Governor. We took care of your problem. The Mandalorians have prevailed. Your reign is secure. It's time for you to uphold your end of the agreement. You've been harboring a murderer. You've even funded his attempts to rebuild the ''Death Watch''. Now, what I want to know is…where is ''Vizsla?"<br />"''He…he left Galidraan!''"<br />"''That's not the right answer. I killed your enemies…now tell me where to find ''mine!|Jango Fett to the governor of Galidraan|<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />}}
[[File:MandosBeatRebels.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The True Mandalorians battle rebel forces on Galidraan]]
Though the Death Watch had caught the True Mandalorians by surprise with their return,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> under Jango Fett's leadership, the True Mandalorians not only made it off of Korda Six with the majority of their forces alive, but went on to hunt the Death Watch across the galaxy.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> Eight years after the events on Korda Six, in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]],<ref name="TEGW" /> the True Mandalorians followed the trail of Death Watch to the planet [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]]<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> in the [[Thanium sector/Legends|Thanium sector]].<ref name="TEAOC" /> Upon discovering rumors that the [[Governor of Galidraan]] was harboring Tor Vizsla and financing his efforts to rebuild the Death Watch, the ''Mand'alor'' Fett arranged to aid the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] in his struggle against a group of [[Galidraanian rebels|local rebels]] in exchange for information on the whereabouts of Vizsla and the Death Watch.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

The True Mandalorians assaulted the Galidraan rebels at their [[Galidraan rebel base|base]], striking from the air with jetpacks before landing and engaging the rebels on the [[Water/Legends|snow]]-covered ground. A short and overwhelming attack, the Galidraan rebels were unable to match the True Mandalorians' blasters, wrist-rockets, and flamerthrowers, and subsequently perished. With their task complete, Fett ordered his troops back to the camp they had established on Galidraan, while Fett himself journeyed to the [[Galidraan governor's palace|governor's palace]] in order to collect their payment. There, Fett met with the governor and informed him of the rebels' defeat, but when he asked for the agreed upon information on Vizsla's whereabouts in return, the governor denied that Vizsla was on Galidraan. At that moment, Vizsla and a cadre of Death Watch soldiers broke from their hiding place within the palace and attacked Fett, forcing the Mandalorian leader to flee the governor's palace via a large window using his jetpack. Vizsla fired his blaster after Fett, striking and damaging his jetpack, and forcing him to crash land in the snow far from the True Mandalorian camp.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

Though the True Mandalorians had succeeded in locating the Death Watch, their hunt only played into yet another trap Death Watch had set for their foes. While the True Mandalorians fought the rebels, the governor of Galidraan had contacted the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]]<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],<ref name="TEA" /> asking for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] aid in dealing with an army of Mandalorians he claimed had been slaughtering political activists. No sooner had Fett uncovered Vizsla's presence on Galidraan,<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> when [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class cruisers]]<ref name="PMICS">''[[Star Wars: Episode I Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref> bearing a [[Jedi task force]] descended upon Galidraan. Vizsla instructed the governor to direct the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] to the True Mandalorians' camp and inform them that Fett's troops were responsible for the murder of innocent women and [[Youngling/Legends|children]]—Death Watch itself would provide the necessary bodies for evidence of the True Mandalorians' supposed crimes. Though Fett was able to spot the Jedi [[Starship/Legends|ships]], his helmet's comlink had been damaged in the fall, forcing Fett to run to the camp through the snow on foot in order to warn his comrades.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

[[File:MandaloriansOnGalidraan-JFOS3.jpg|thumb|right|325px|On Galidraan, the True Mandalorians clashed with a Jedi task force due the machinations of the Death Watch]]
Jango Fett arrived at the True Mandalorian camp only moments before the Jedi.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> The task force, led by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], took the lying governor at his word<ref name="TCWn">[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' novelization]]</ref> and demanded the Mandalorians' immediate surrender in order to bring them into custody on the charge of murder, with Dooku's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Komari Vosa]] adding that should they resist, the Mandalorians would be killed.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> Innocent<ref name="Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> and indignant, Fett ordered the True Mandalorians to open fire on the Jedi, specifically targeting Vosa. Though their blasters were effective at holding the Jedi at bay, the energy bolts that were reflected by the Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] proved dangerous and costly to the True Mandalorian ranks, and Fett instead had his soldiers switch to harder to deflect projectile weapons. As True Mandalorians launched volleys of wrist-fired rockets at their Jedi foes, Fett ordered several jetpack-equipped soldiers to provide air support and cover for the troops on the ground. A number of Jedi fell to the attacks, and numerous Mandalorians perished either to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] or the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|slash]] of a Jedi's lightsaber. An airborne True Mandalorians by the name of [[Myles]] was [[Sai tok/Legends|cut down]] in midair by a [[Unidentified Jedi (Myles' killer)|Jedi's]] blade while Fett could only watch. During the melee, Fett personally killed six Jedi with no weapon beyond his own [[Limb/Legends|hands]], feet, and armored body; the Jedi responsible for Myles' death was disarmed and [[Keldabe kiss/Legends|violently subdued]] by Fett, before the Mandalorian leader finally ended his life with a [[Fibercord/Legends|fibercord]] [[Garrote/Legends|garrote]].<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />

Despite his efforts, Fett couldn't change the outcome of the battle: though the Jedi's forces had been halved—eleven of their original twenty slain by the True Mandalorians<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>—every True Mandalorian on Galidraan, save for Fett himself, was killed by the Jedi. With his comrades dead, a defeated and emotionally exhausted Fett fell to his knees in the Galidraan snow and allowed the Jedi to take him into custody.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> He was stripped of his Mandalorian armor and turned over to the authority of Galidraan's governor, who subsequently sold Fett into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] while keeping his armor as a treasured prize.<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> The Mandalorian Civil War had come to an end, and through their deceitful machinations, Death Watch triumphed over the True Mandalorians.<ref name="HotM" />

Without the active opposition of the True Mandalorians to challenge them,<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> the Death Watch was free to move against the New Mandalorians. While Fett was enslaved,<ref name="TBHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref> Death Watch incited a bloody conflict on Mandalore that grew to be known as the [[Great Clan Wars/Legends|Great Clan Wars]]. This second civil war divided numerous clans and pitted family members against one another,<ref name="ShadowConspiracy" /> as some supported Death Watch's aims, while others believed in the ideals of the True Mandalorians.<ref name="TEGW" /> Many Mandalorians died over the course of the conflict,<ref name="VoT">{{TCW|Voyage of Temptation}}</ref> but Death Watch ultimately failed to unseat the New Mandalorian regime. Several of the [[Chief/Legends|chieftain]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who had been swayed to war by Death Watch went into exile on Mandalore's [[Moon/Legends|moon]], [[Concordia/Legends|Concordia]],<ref name="ShadowConspiracy" /> along with several defeated Death Watch warriors. Tor Vizsla and those Death Watch troops who still followed him retreated to regroup and rebuild.<ref name="TBHC" />

In his training manifesto entitled ''[[Ba'jurne Kyr'tsad Mando'ad]]'', Death Watch [[Secret Mandalore|leader]] [[Tor Vizsla]] instructed his [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] to watch for the signs of the True Mandalorian remnants rebuilding themselves. He insisted that they must remember that those who escaped Galidraan were as familiar with the Mandalorian principle of ''ba'slan shev'la'',<ref name="TBHC" /> or "strategic disappearance,"<ref name="TrueColors">''[[Republic Commando: True Colors]]''</ref> as they were.<ref name="TBHC" />

====The last hunt of the True Mandalorians====
{{Quote|The armor's right here, governor—and I'm going to use it to remove your lungs, unless you tell me where to find Vizsla and the Death Watch.|Jango Fett, preparing to hunt down the Death Watch|<ref name="OpenSeasons4" />}}
For two years, Jango Fett lived as a slave aboard a [[Transport/Legends|spice transport]], until the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] that transport came under attack by a group of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. During the chaos of the attack, Fett freed himself and killed his [[Slaver/Legends|captors]], before fighting his way off of the transport. Traveling back to Galidraan, Fett infiltrated the governor's palace and recovered the armor that had been taken from him, before demanding the location of the Death Watch from the governor on the threat of death. From there, Fett tracked Tor Vizsla's ship,<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> the ''[[Death Rattle]]'',<ref name="DeathRattle">{{FactFile|124|VIZ 1-4|Vizsla}}</ref> to the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. Assaulting the vessel with a [[Jango Fett's starfighter|starfighter]] of his own, Fett destroyed the ''Death Rattle''{{'s}} [[Engine/Legends|engines]] in an unmanned ramming maneuver, while he personally blew his way into the ship through the ''Rattle''{{'s}} forward [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] using a pair of wrist-launched rockets and only his armor for protection against the blast and the vacuum of space.<ref name="OpenSeasons4" />

[[File:Jangovsvizsla.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Jango Fett [[Showdown on Corellia|battled]] Tor Vizsla in a final, fierce confrontation on Corellia]]
Once aboard the crippled ''Death Rattle'', Fett pursued a fleeing Vizsla to the vessel's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], where the two engaged in a brief bout of unarmed combat that saw Vizsla temporarily victorious. Yet as the Death Watch leader moved to board an escape pod, Fett leaped after him, and their battle was taken from orbit down to the surface of Corellia. Emerging from the pod, Fett and Vizsla fought each other fiercely—their efforts attracted the attention of a pack of nearby [[dire-cat]]s—before Vizsla finally stabbed Fett in the torso with a [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] blade from his gauntlet. Though the toxin was nonlethal, it sickened Fett and rendered him unable to defend himself against Vizsla, who used the opportunity to beat and berate his foe, all the while oblivious to the dire-cats that had moved in closer and caught Fett's notice. Before Vizsla could deal the killing blow, Fett slashed the Death Watch leader across his stomach, cutting between the gap in his armor's plates and drawing a substantial amount of [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. The scent of the blood caused the dire-cats to finally attack, mauling and killing Vizsla, while leaving Fett unharmed due to the smell of the poison in his bloodstream. With the death of Tor Vizsla, Fett had succeeded in avenging his fallen True Mandalorian comrades,<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> and finally completed the work of his surrogate father, Jaster Mereel.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I believed my Masters. I didn't think for myself. They didn't question, either; they took the governor at his word. They just ''believed''. And we killed people. We killed them on the say-so of a criminal.|Count Dooku, reflecting on his role in the destruction of the True Mandalorians|<ref name="TCWn" />}}
The loss of his comrades and his time as a slave made Jango Fett a bitter man, and he grew distant from the rest of the Mandalorian people and his role as ''Mand'alor''.<ref name="O66" /> With the True Mandalorians gone, Fett turned to the solitary life of a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]].<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> The destruction of the True Mandalorians at Galidraan haunted Jedi Master Dooku for years after.<ref name="TCWn" /> While at the time he had been appalled by the carnage that had occurred,<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> years later he would privately consider it a turning point in his life, the event that caused him to begin questioning the decisions of the Jedi Council, for Dooku and his Jedi task force had killed the True Mandalorians on the basis of a lie told to the Council by the corrupt<ref name="TCWn" /> Galidraanian governor.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> The events of Galidraan contributed in part to Dooku's decision to leave the Jedi Order, and he eventually fell under the sway of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], becoming his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Tyranus.<ref name="JvS" /> Having seen the ferocity of Mandalorian soldiers firsthand, Tyranus captured the only living True Mandalorian beside Jango Fett, a soldier by the name of [[Silas (Mandalorian)|Silas]], who had been absent from the battle on Galidraan and thus survived the massacre there. With the information on Fett he [[Torture/Legends|obtained]] from Silas before killing the man, Tyranus recruited Fett to serve as the [[Clone template/Legends|progenitor]] of a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] meant to one day destroy the Jedi. Fett claimed the first [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] for himself,<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> a child whom he named [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba]] and raised as a son, passing on the True Mandalorian teachings of Jaster Mereel<ref name="HotM" /> until his [[Sai cha/Legends|death]] at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]].<ref name="AotC">{{Film|II}}</ref>

[[File:AlmecDeathWatch-ShadesOfReason.png|thumb|right|300px|Without the True Mandalorians to oppose them, the Death Watch was able to succeed in their goals of conquering the pacifistic New Mandalorians during the Clone Wars]]
The scattered Death Watch<ref name="HotM" /> carried on with their plans to bring about the downfall of the pacifist New Mandalorians in secret,<ref name="ShadowConspiracy" /> free of the True Mandalorians' interference.<ref name="TBHC" /> Over time, Death Watch rebuilt their forces in hiding,<ref name="NewBattlefronts">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: New Battlefronts: The Visual Guide]]''</ref> finally emerging at the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] to [[Attack on the Memorial Shrine/Legends|again]]<ref name="TMP">{{TCW|The Mandalore Plot}}</ref> [[Plot to assassinate Satine Kryze|strike]] at the New Mandalorians,<ref name="VoT" /> and in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], the rogue Mandalorian sect was finally successful in [[Takeover of Mandalore/Legends|bringing down]] the New Mandalorian faction.<ref name="ShadowConspiracy" /><ref name="TLJ">[[The Last Jedi (novel)|''The Last Jedi'']]</ref>

When [[Spar]]—an [[Alpha-class Advanced Recon Commando|ARC trooper]] deserter from the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone]] army Jango Fett had spawned for the Republic—succeeded his progenitor as ''Mand'alor'', and proceeded to raise an army of Mandalorian supercommandos, his new [[Mandalorian Protectors (Spar)|Mandalorian Protectors]] were formed in the image of the True Mandalorians. Like the True Mandalorians who came before, the Protectors placed an importance on high moral standards, and followed the teachings of Jaster Mereel's Supercommando Codex. It was also the destruction of the True Mandalorians at the hands of the Jedi on Galidraan,<ref name="GaW" /> in addition to the oppressive nature of the Republic,<ref name="HotM" /> that prompted Spar to align the Mandalorians with the Confederacy of Independent Systems during the Clone Wars.<ref name="GaW" />

Despite the relatively short time span of their existence, the True Mandalorians made a significant impact upon the historical records of the galaxy. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] Historical Council detailed the rise and fall of the True Mandalorians over the course of the Mandalorian Civil War in their historical chronicle entitled [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|''The New Essential Chronology'']].<ref name="NEC" /> The [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] briefly mentioned the True Mandalorians in her compendium known as [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''The Essential Guide to the Force'']],<ref name="JvS" /> and the historical [[Historian/Legends|scholar]] [[Vilnau Teupt]] discussed the True Mandalorians during his keynote address at the our-hundred-twelfth [[Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History]] at the [[Brentaal Academy]], entitled "[[Industry, Honor, Savagery: Shaping the Mandalorian Soul]]."<ref name="TEGW" />

==Notable True Mandalorians==
===Jaster Mereel===
{{Main|Jaster Mereel}}
[[File:Jaster_Closeup.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jaster Mereel, founder and leader of the True Mandalorians]]
{{Quote|Now, Death Watch. Tried to oust Jaster Mereel because he liked law and order, and that crimped their game.|[[Nyreen Vollen]]|<ref name="ImpCom501st" />}}
A native of Concord Dawn and a member of the world's law-keeping Journeyman Protectors, Jaster Mereel was an extraordinary soldier and a respected man of strong morals, morals that drove Mereel to kill a corrupt superior officer in an act that saw him banished from his home. Mereel later joined the Mandalorians, and sensing a general dissatisfaction with the decline in ethics among the more dishonorable members of the warrior culture, he fought to become the new ''Mand'alor'' and bring a new ideal to the clans,<ref name="HotM" /> beating out the barbaric Tor Vizsla in the process.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> With the Supercommando Codex,<ref name="HotM" /> Mereel declared that any Mandalorian who wished to fight would do so as honorable, highly-paid soldiers.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> Opposed by the pacifistic New Mandalorian government,<ref name="TEA" /> and Vizsla's own radical followers calling themselves the Death Watch, civil war broke out between the violent Death Watch and Mereel's own loyal soldiers, who proclaimed themselves the "True Mandalorians."<ref name="HotM" /> Mereel led his True Mandalorian soldiers against the Death Watch into battle on his home planet, where his troops were saved by the young Jango Fett, and managed to severely wound Vizsla in a skirmish that saw the Death Watch defeated. His family murdered by Death Watch, Mereel became a surrogate father to Fett, and inducted the boy into the Mandalorian culture.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />

In the belief that the Death Watch was no more following their defeat on Concord Dawn, Mereel took to leading the True Mandalorians as a mercenary army. In 58 BBY, Mereel and the True Mandalorians were contracted to extract an inexperienced security team from hostile fire on Korda Six. With Kordan intelligence suggesting minimal resistance, Mereel viewed the mission as "easy credits" and brought his army in, only to find that an ambush had been laid by the Death Watch. When Mereel's second-in-command, Montross, refused to obey the ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} order to retreat and found himself surrounded by enemy forces, Mereel was forced to rescue his disobedient subordinate, only to come under fire by Tor Vizsla himself. Montross abandoned the Mandalorian leader, using his jetpack to escape Vizsla's assault while Mereel was left to perish. Jango Fett attempted to rescue his adoptive father, but could not reach the ''Mand'alor'' in time; mortally wounded, Mereel died in the arms of his surrogate son, and Fett returned the fallen ''Mand'alor''{{'s}} body to the True Mandalorians' extraction point. There Montross argued his claim the ''Mand'alor'' title, but when the truth of his betrayal was exposed, the True Mandalorians rallied to Fett as their new leader. Montross slipped into exile,<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> as Fett succeeded Mereel as both ''Mand'alor''<ref name="HotM" /> and the leader of the True Mandalorians in accordance with what his troops believed Mereel would have wanted.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" />

===Jango Fett===
{{Main|Jango Fett/Legends}}
[[File:JangoSansHelmet-SWE.png|thumb|right|190px|Jango Fett, the second leader of the True Mandalorians]]
{{Quote|You were ''at'' Galidraan…tell me, how did one mere man kill so many of your kind?''"<br />"''With his bare hands.|Darth Sidious and Darth Tyranus|<ref name="OpenSeasons3" />}}
The son of a Journeyman Protector, Jango Fett grew up on his family's farm on Concord Dawn. His quiet farming lifestyle was uprooted when he was still only a child, however, when the civil war between the True Mandalorians and Death Watch spilled onto his home planet. Fett's family was destroyed by Tor Vizsla and his Death Watch soldiers, and he only escaped their fate due to the intervention of Jaster Mereel,<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> ''Mand'alor'' and leader of the True Mandalorians.<ref name="HotM" /> Fett was taken in by Mereel, who raised the boy as his surrogate son and training him to be a Mandalorian. When the True Mandalorians were contracted to rescue a security team under fire on Korda Six, Fett was given his first command role; at the age of fourteen<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />—now an adult in Mandalorian society<ref name="TMPaC" />—Fett led an infantry unit of True Mandalorian troops on Korda Six, where they discovered the entire mission had been launched on a ruse perpetrated by the Death Watch. Overwhelmed by the native Kordans, and succumbing to a Death Watch ambush, the True Mandalorian forces suffered heavy casualties, including the death of Jaster Mereel. With the backing of the other True Mandalorians, Fett succeeded his surrogate father as leader of the True Mandalorians<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> and ''Mand'alor''.<ref name="HotM" />

Under Fett's leadership, the True Mandalorians struck back at the Death Watch, hunting Tor Vizsla to the Outer Rim world of Galidraan. Fett and his soldiers put down a local rebellion against the governor of Galidraan in exchange for information on Vizsla's whereabouts, but when Fett went to collect on the governor's part of the agreement, he was ambushed in the governor's palace by Vizsla and a cadre of Death Watch troops. At the same time, a Jedi task force touched down on Galidraan, seeking to arrest the True Mandalorians due to lies they had been fed by the governor as Vizsla's proxy. The True Mandalorians resisted and a battle between their forces and those of the Jedi broke out;<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> half of the Jedi strike team was slain,<ref name="JvS" /> six of whom were felled by Fett's own hand, but by the battle's end, the True Mandalorians on Galidraan had been destroyed, with Fett as the only survivor.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> Subdued by the Jedi and delivered into the custody of Galidraan's governor, Fett was subsequently sold into slavery, until escaping two years later to finish the True Mandalorians' work and eliminate the Death Watch. Tracking Death Watch to Corellia, he crippled their ship and clashed with Vizsla in a prolonged fight that moved from orbit to the surface of Corellia. There, he critically wounded the Death Watch leader, and Vizsla was devoured by a pack of nearby dire-cats that were drawn by the smell of blood. With the True Mandalorians gone,<ref name="OpenSeasons4" /> Fett grew distant from the rest of the Mandalorian people and his role as ''Mand'alor'',<ref name="O66" /> assuming a solitary life as a lone bounty hunter until his death.<ref name="AotC" />

===Montross===
{{Main|Montross (bounty hunter)}}
[[File:Montross_1a.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Montross, True Mandalorian soldier and betrayer]]
{{Quote|Montross! Airlift! Now!''"<br />"''Sorry, Jaster. I'm through taking your orders. But I'll take good care of the troops.|Montross betrays Jaster Mereel and leaves him to die on Korda Six|<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />}}
A Mandalorian soldier with a fearsome reputation,<ref name="BHManual">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'' manual</ref> when the Death Watch formed in opposition to Jaster Mereel, Montross was one of many who rallied to the ''Mand'alor'', proclaiming themselves to be the True Mandalorians.<ref name="HotM" /> Montross was one of several Mandalorian hardliners who demanded Mereel take definitive action against the Death Watch,<ref name="TCSWE" /> and as civil war broke out, Montross followed Mereel into battle against Death Watch. On Concord Dawn, Montross took part in an ambush that devastated Death Watch forces and nearly saw the death of the rogue sect's leader, Tor Vizsla; even as the victorious True Mandalorians made to depart the Outer Rim world, Montross lagged behind, shooting down fleeing Death Watch stragglers until Mereel ordered him to stand down.<ref name="OpenSeasons1" /> Montross and Mereel were often at odds over issues of tactics and Montross' own tendency to inflict levels of collateral damage that Mereel found unacceptable.<ref name="BHManual" />

Years later, during the True Mandalorians mission to extract a security team under fire from local hostiles on Korda Six, Montross was placed in command of Vertigo Company, a unit of jetpack-equipped troops tasked with providing air support for True Mandalorian forces on the ground. Though the True Mandalorians incurred severe casualties due to the overwhelming hostile Kordans, Montross and Vertigo Company pressed their attack, disobeying Jaster Mereel's orders to fall back. Mereel personally rescued Montross from the Kordans, but when the Death Watch revealed their presence on the planet and attacked the True Mandalorians, Montross left Mereel to die to enemy fire, having grown tired of taking orders from him. Falling back to the True Mandalorians' landing zone, Montross insisted the Mandalorians retreat in accordance with Mereel's last order, and presented himself as the best choice to succeed Mereel as leader. However, when Jango Fett—Mereel's surrogate son and fellow True Mandalorian—arrived back at the extraction point carrying Mereel's body, he revealed Montross' betrayal. The True Mandalorians rallied behind Fett<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> and appointed him the new ''Mand'alor'',<ref name="HotM" /> while Montross was forced into exile. Banished from Mandalorian society,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> Montross became a bounty hunter known for his ruthlessness. He later [[The Hunt|competed]] with Fett for a bounty on the leader of the [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]] cult<ref name="BountyHunter">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref> after Fett, too, became a bounty hunter following the destruction of the True Mandalorians on Galidraan.<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> In a final confrontation between the former allies-turned-enemies, Fett defeated Montross on [[Bogden/Legends|Bogden's]] moon of [[Kohlma]], and left him to be torn apart by mindless Bando Gora cultists.<ref name="BountyHunter" />

===Silas===
{{Main|Silas (Mandalorian)}}
[[File:Silas_Closeup.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Silas]]
{{Quote|I'll follow Jango. And no one else.|Silas, following the death of Jaster Mereel|<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />}}
A Human male Mandalorian, Silas grew up among the forces of the True Mandalorians. At a young age, he maintained a friendship with Jango Fett, the young surrogate son of ''Mand'alor'' Jaster Mereel, whom he respected and admired greatly. When the True Mandalorians were contracted to extract a security team pinned down under heavy fire on Korda Six, Silas and Fett fought alongside each other in the fierce battle that ensued, moving to rescue the security officers while the bulk of the True Mandalorians engaged the native Kordan warriors. At the extraction site, however, Silas and the other members of his unit were ambushed by a resurgent contingent of the Death Watch splinter group; though Silas was unfamiliar with the group, having joined with the True Mandalorians some time after the Death Watch's assumed destruction on Concord Dawn, he quickly adapted and engaged their new enemy.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

As Jaster Mereel came under attack by the Death Watch, Jango Fett rushed to his adopted father's aid, with Silas close behind. Upon reaching the battlefield, the two soldiers found that they were too late: in a hail of cannon fire from an armored tank, Death Watch leader Tor Vizsla struck down Mereel, while Silas was forced to restrain Fett from running to Mereel's side and joining the ''Mand'alor'' in death. When Vizsla departed, satisfied that Mereel was dead, Fett and Silas recovered Mereel's body, and returned with it to the True Mandalorians' landing zone. There, they found Mereel's traitorous second in command, Montross, attempting to succeed Mereel as the new ''Mand'alor''. Fett objected, on the grounds that Montross had abandoned Mereel to die alone on the battlefield, and Silas rallied to his comrade, insisting that if anyone should take Mereel's place, it should be Fett. With the backing of Silas and the other True Mandalorians, Fett became the new leader of their group,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> as well as the new ''Mand'alor''.<ref name="HotM" /> Silas followed Fett faithfully,<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> but when the True Mandalorians were destroyed at Galidraan,<ref name="OpenSeasons3" /> Silas was absent from the battle. Years later, he was captured by the former Jedi Master Dooku and brutally [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] for information on Jango Fett. Upon finally relenting and relating his time with Fett to Dooku, Silas was subsequently killed.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The True Mandalorians first appeared in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in the [[2002]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']], written by [[W. Haden Blackman]] and illustrated by [[Ramón F. Bachs]]. ''Open Seasons'', which focused on the early life of Jango Fett and his induction into the Mandalorian culture, featured the True Mandalorians in all four issues of the series.<ref name="OpenSeasons">''[[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons]]''</ref> The True Mandalorians were later referenced in the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'', which was released [[December 5]], 2002.<ref name="BountyHunter" /> The organization and its actions were mentioned in [[Karen Traviss]]' [[2009]] novel ''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]'',<ref name="ImpCom501st" /> and again in [[James Luceno]]'s novel [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']], published in [[2012]].<ref name="Plagueis" />

Further details on the True Mandalorians and their place within the larger Mandalorian culture were provided by the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], by [[Abel G. Peña]].<ref name="HotM" /> The informational entry for the Mandalorian Protectors in the ''[[Galaxy at War]]'' sourcebook referenced the True Mandalorians,<ref name="GaW" /> as did ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'',<ref name="NEC" /> and the online role-playing guide for the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]], entitled ''<span class="plainlinks">[https://web.archive.org/web/20100523212919/http://starwarsgalaxies.station.sony.com/players/guides.vm?id=80130 The Way of the Warrior: A Guide to becoming a Mandalorian Soldier]</span>''.<ref name="WotW">{{SonyCite|url=players/guides.vm?id=80130|text=The Way of the Warrior: A Guide to becoming a Mandalorian Soldier}}</ref> As part of their detailing of the history of the planet Mandalore and the Mandalorian culture, both ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''<ref name="TEA" /> and ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' mentioned the True Mandalorians.<ref name="TEGW" />

During a discussion in Karen Traviss' ''Republic Commando'' novel ''Order 66'', the veteran Mandalorian soldiers [[Kal Skirata]] and [[Walon Vau]] note that they both should have been with Jango Fett at the battle on Galidraan.<ref name="O66" /> Though this would suggest that Skirata and Vau were affiliated with the True Mandalorians, or felt some connection to the group, neither character has been canonically linked with the True Mandalorians at this point in time.

A Mandalorian banner in the [[Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities]] bears the sigil of the group in the new ''[[Star Wars]]'' canon.<ref name="GEstaff">{{GEStaff|Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities|store}}</ref>
